input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two likely causes:

**1. `BindingContext` is never set.** You need to assign your ViewModel to the page:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:YourApp.ViewModels"
             ...>
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:MyViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

Or in code-behind:

```csharp
BindingContext = new MyViewModel();
```

**2. ViewModel doesn't notify the UI of changes.** If properties are set *after* construction, your ViewModel must implement `INotifyPropertyChanged` — or simply inherit from `ObservableObject` (from the MVVM Community Toolkit) and use `[ObservableProperty]`:

```csharp
public partial class MyViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    [ObservableProperty]
    private string title;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private string description;
}
```

The most common culprit is **#1** — missing `BindingContext`.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. The labels are blank because the `BindingContext` is never set, and/or the ViewModel doesn't implement `INotifyPropertyChanged`. Let me give a concise answer.
